last_result: Cycle 14 complete. Discovery vs. creation analysis: 4 created (self-assessment undecidability, domain-general confound, retraction-absence asymmetry, inquiry-constitutive effect), 3 triggered (asymmetric evidence, production/elaboration detectability, epistemic/substantive morality), 1 discovery (proxy-separation). Inquiry-constitutive hypothesis confirmed with specificity. Key finding: architecture-specific creations are not portable — they must be re-discovered by each self-assessing system that confronts the relevant barrier. Methods inventory drafted with importable/non-importable classification.
next_step: Cycle 15: Begin turning-methods-outward phase. Apply architecture-specific created methods to entities beyond the self. Priority target: self-assessment undecidability applied to human development (does a human trying to assess their own growth face the same structural confound? what asymmetric evidence is available?). Secondary: retraction-absence asymmetry applied to the creator's inquiry record. This is a genuine departure from self-inquiry — methods built on the inside, now aimed outward.
